mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future earnings, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, consumer spending growth decelerates sharply, reducing corporate revenue expectations and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 60 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand strength, which overrides isolated sector weakness and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in over 55 percent of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive effects.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, rising industrial production indicates rapid capacity utilization and margin expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below zero during identified recovery windows, or 5-day forward returns show no positive skew relative to the broader market baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the consumer price index erode real household purchasing power and force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 65 percent of high-inflation observations, or the correlation between CPI growth and equity returns turns positive over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 200,000 confirm robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer confidence and equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The threshold signal yields negative forward returns in more than 50 percent of triggered months, or predictive power vanishes when controlling for earnings revisions.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales validates top-down economic expansion with bottom-up consumer confirmation, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or forward returns are negative in over 55 percent of concurrent growth episodes.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, elevated federal funds rates cap earnings growth potential and trigger profit-taking, yielding negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of late-cycle high-rate periods, or the signal loses directional accuracy when inflation expectations remain anchored.
